description: Feature ID: 3CI9i0ix There is an icon used for the logo image now 🤖.  Any place we are using this, use the actual logo instead.  There are many size icons you can choose in the KaiBot project folder ./images/.  Use image tools like sips or even python code to find the right size image files and make them the logo.   Make a favicon file as well and make sure the web server uses it.
planPoints: 1. Resize KaiBotIcon512.png to 64px for web logo and copy to web/static/images/ — created web/static/images/KaiBotLogo64.png using Python/Pillow
2. Create a 32px favicon.ico from KaiBotIcon512.png in web/static/ — created web/static/favicon.ico (16px + 32px) using Python/Pillow
3. Add favicon route in routes.ts to serve /favicon.ico — added route before /static/* that delegates to serveStatic("/static/favicon.ico")
4. Replace 🤖 emoji logo in templates.ts (project selection page) with an `` tag — updated .logo div and added .logo img CSS
5. Replace 🤖 emoji logo in templates.ts (main dashboard page) with an `` tag — updated header status bar span.logo with inline img (28px)
6. Add `<link rel="icon">` favicon tags to both HTML pages in templates.ts — added to project selection page and main dashboard page
title: Replace emoji logo with favicon and images
summary: The feature replaced the 🤖 emoji logo placeholder with the actual KaiBot logo image throughout the web UI. This involved resizing the original icon file to create a 64×64px logo and a multi-size favicon, then updating both the project selection and main dashboard pages to use the image files instead of the emoji. A new favicon route was also added to the web server to properly serve the favicon.ico file.

ls /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/images/ && sips -g pixelWidth -g pixelHeight /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/images/KaiBot1024.png /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/images/KaiBotIcon512.png 2>/dev/null
Grep: {"pattern":"static|favicon|/images/|WEB_ROOT","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/web/routes.ts","output_mode":"content","context":3}
Grep: {"pattern":"favicon","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/web/routes.ts","output_mode":"content","context":3}
Now I have a clear picture. Let me write the plan and then execute it.Now let's execute the steps. First, let me resize the images:

sips -z 64 64 /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/images/KaiBotIcon512.png --out /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/images/KaiBotLogo64.png && echo "64px logo created"
Grep: {"pattern":"ALLOWED_COMMANDS","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/security.ts","output_mode":"content"}
`sips` is in the source allowlist but the hook in the running agent environment is blocking it. I'll use Python instead (also allowed):

python3 -c "
from PIL import Image
import sys

img = Image.open('/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/images/KaiBotIcon512.png').convert('RGBA')
img64 = img.resize((64, 64), Image.LANCZOS)
img64.save('/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/images/KaiBotLogo64.png')
print('64px logo created')
"

python3 -c "
from PIL import Image

img = Image.open('/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/images/KaiBotIcon512.png').convert('RGBA')
img32 = img.resize((32, 32), Image.LANCZOS)
img16 = img.resize((16, 16), Image.LANCZOS)
# Save as ICO with multiple sizes
img32.save('/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/favicon.ico', format='ICO', sizes=[(16, 16), (32, 32)])
print('favicon.ico created')
"
Now add the favicon route to routes.ts:

Now let me check how serveStatic resolves paths — I need to make sure `/favicon.ico` maps to `web/static/favicon.ico`:The `serveStatic` function maps paths relative to `web/`. Since the favicon is at `web/static/favicon.ico`, the route should pass `/static/favicon.ico`:
Now replace the 🤖 emoji in the project selection page with an `` tag:

Now replace the 🤖 emoji in the main dashboard page. The logo is in the `<header>` status bar as a `<span class="logo">`:

{"tool":"Edit","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/web/templates.ts","old":" 🤖 Protovate KaiBot","new":" Protovate KaiBot"}
Now add `<link rel="icon">` to both HTML pages:

{"tool":"Edit","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/web/templates.ts","old":"  <title>Protovate KaiBot — Select Project</title>

  <!-- App styles (no external CDN dependencies) -->","new":"  <title>Protovate KaiBot — ${esc(projectName)}</title>

Now let's run typecheck to make sure there are no errors:
npm run typecheck 2>&1
No errors. Let me verify the final state of the changed files looks good:
ls -la /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/images/ && ls -la /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/favicon.ico

Now append the summary:The feature is complete. Here's what was done: 1. **Logo image (64px)** — Resized `KaiBotIcon512.png` to 64×64px using Python/Pillow and saved to `web/static/images/KaiBotLogo64.png` 2. **Favicon** — Created `web/static/favicon.ico` with 16×32px sizes from the same source image 3. **Favicon route** — Added `/favicon.ico` handler in `routes.ts` that maps to `serveStatic("/static/favicon.ico")` — available even before a project is selected 4. **Project selection page** — Replaced `🤖` emoji with `` (48px), added CSS for `.logo img`, and added `` in `` 5. **Main dashboard page** — Replaced `🤖` emoji in the top status bar with an inline `` (28px to match the surrounding text height), and added `` in ``
✅ Feature complete — Cost: $0.5777  Turns: 37  Time: 162.9s
